pipeline outperforms the No Inference Baseline significantly.

To accommodate
human preferences from conversations, our method uses the best of both worlds and is data efficient
and is generalizable for a broad set of tasks and domains.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

aspects of being data efficient, can be generalized across many domains and tasks, and is online.

concepts.finding:
The “search inventory” function uses the NLTK
package [37] and a Kaggle Grocery Store inventory dataset [47] to implement a simple embedding
search by similarity, and retrieves a single most similar item.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
We demonstrated that our pipeline can efficiently propose and add new goals based
on conversations with the human, and remove goals that are deemed unlikely, undesirable, or unsafe.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
While offline methods like RLHF can represent various goals but require
large datasets, our approach achieves similar flexibility with online efficiency.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
2
Background and Relevant Work
For robots/embodied agents that can achieve difficult tasks while interacting with humans (ie:
household tasks), language grounding for robots is important – that robots can understand language
and make connections between language and its physical environment.
